evidence: webapp/index.html line 611: fetch(`${apiUrl}/api/semantic-field/examples?adjective=${...}&noun=${...}&max=${max}`) — server handler (ExplorationHandlers.java:194-197) requires params 'collocate', 'seed', 'top'; the webapp sends old names 'adjective', 'noun', 'max', causing requireParam('collocate') to throw a 400 for every examples call in the comparison panel

ExplorationHandlers.java line 131 Javadoc: 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field' — but WordSketchApiServer registers no such route; it was removed in a prior cleanup commit (5666688) and the claim is now false

RelationType.java: four constants (ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF) explicitly documented as 'no longer set in any grammar config'; GrammarConfigLoader.parseRelationType() accepts them silently; they exist only to avoid breaking tests — dead production constants retained in the enum

ExplorationResult 5-arg constructor (line 40-43) delegates to the 6-arg constructor with Map.of() for perSeedCollocates; ExploreResponseAssembler has an explicit 'Legacy fallback for ExplorationResult instances without per-seed data' branch (line 49) that fires exclusively for test-created instances, never in production

strengths: The codebase has undergone active cleanup of legacy artifacts: the old /api/semantic-field route was removed, AdjectiveProfile was renamed to CollocateProfile, and a startup deprecation warning was eliminated — desloppify commit 5666688 shows organised migration work
Legacy RelationType constants are clearly documented in the class Javadoc with explicit 'Not set in grammar config' labels on each constant, preventing silent confusion
GrammarConfigLoader.validateNoLegacyKeys() proactively rejects old 'copulas' config key with an actionable error message, preventing old-format configs from loading silently
stale_migration.issue_character: Issues are migration artifacts from a partially-completed API rename: the server-side parameters were updated but the client (webapp) and some documentation were not, leaving one broken feature and one stale doc claim alongside genuinely dead enum constants.
stale_migration.score_rationale: The webapp API mismatch (wrong param names for /api/semantic-field/examples) is a concrete broken feature — the comparison panel examples call will always receive a 400 error because requireParam('collocate') fails. This pulls the score below 70. The stale Javadoc and dead RelationType constants are minor but real migration residue. Against global anchors: 80 = solid but uneven, 60 = significant drag. The codebase is clearly in an active migration posture with good cleanup, but one broken client path and surviving dead-code constants place it between these anchors at 62.

issues.summary: webapp calls /api/semantic-field/examples with old param names, always returns 400
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.java lines 194-197: handler requires 'collocate' and 'seed' via requireParam(), and reads 'top' (not 'max') for the limit — sending 'adjective'/'noun'/'max' will trigger a 400 Bad Request on every invocation
issues.suggestion: Update the fetch call in webapp/index.html line 611 to use the current parameter names: `?collocate=${encodeURIComponent(adj)}&seed=${encodeURIComponent(noun)}&top=${max}` (also update any local variable names from adj/noun to collocate/headword for clarity)

issues.summary: ExplorationHandlers Javadoc claims /api/semantic-field legacy path still exists but it was removed
evidence:
ExplorationHandlers.java line 131: '

Also reachable at the legacy path {@code /api/semantic-field}.

'
Git commit 5666688 message: 'Remove deprecated /api/semantic-field route and startup warning'
issues.suggestion:
Remove the sentence '

Also reachable at the legacy path {@code /api/semantic-field}.

' from the handleSemanticFieldComparison Javadoc in ExplorationHandlers.java line 131

issues.summary: RelationType enum retains 4 dead legacy constants (ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF) only referenced by tests
evidence: RelationType.java lines 22-29: four constants labeled 'Not set in grammar config' in their Javadoc

Class Javadoc (line 11-15): 'legacy labels from an earlier exploration subsystem design... Priority-hint usages in the exploration layer were removed — only the fallback (POS-group matching) fires in practice'
grep shows no production code references to ADJ_PREDICATE/ADJ_MODIFIER/SUBJECT_OF/OBJECT_OF in src/main/java (only in src/test/java)
issues.suggestion: Remove ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF from RelationType; update the three test references in RelationPatternBuilderTest and RelationUtilsTest to construct RelationConfig instances with RelationType.SURFACE or RelationType.DEP, removing the dependency on the dead constants

root_causes: API parameter rename (adjective/noun/max → collocate/seed/top) was applied to the server-side handler but not propagated to the webapp client, a classic partial-migration artifact
Removal of the /api/semantic-field legacy route was not accompanied by a Javadoc update in the handler that still references it
likely_symptoms: webapp-examples-stale-params is the root failure; stale-javadoc-legacy-path is a documentation symptom of the same removal operation
